Exit criteria: (1) all 15 child issues closed; (2) R CMD check clean; (3) all Phase 1 tests pass including integration on bench suites; (4) infer() round-trips from a real prcomp fit AND a real multivarious::pca fit (when installed) AND the reference cross adapter for both covariance and correlation; (5) variable_stability, score_stability, subspace_stability tables populated and keyed by unit_id; (6) notes/vitale_improvements.md Part 5 section 39 Phase 1 section updated with COMPLETE marker; (7) git commit landed.
acceptance_criteria: all child issues closed; R CMD check OK; integration tests pass; notes updated; commit pushed
close_reason: PHASE 1 EXIT GATE PASSED. All 15 child issues closed. Exit criteria verified: (1) R CMD check OK across the full Phase 1 suite (0/0/0); (2) infer() round-trips on real adapter_prcomp / cross_svd / multivarious_pca / multivarious_plsc; (3) variable, score, and subspace stability tables populated and keyed by unit_id; (4) cost block records wall_time_phases; (5) mc block records seed and exceedance counts; (6) integration tests pass on all 4 Phase 0 bench suites; (7) notes/vitale_improvements.md Part 5 section 39 Phase 1 marked COMPLETE with delivery manifest. title: Freeze infer_result schema (unit-centered)
description: Define the frozen output schema for every infer() call. See notes §37 (latent units) and §44 (unit-centered schema). Fields: units (with unit_id, unit_type in {component,subspace}, members, identifiable, selected), component_tests, variable_stability, score_stability, subspace_stability, assumptions, mc (reproducibility log per §41), cost (from §32), provenance. Implement as an S3 class infer_result with constructor, validator, and stub print/summary methods. Every downstream table keys off unit_id, not raw component index. This schema is FROZEN at the end of Phase 0 — no fields added without explicit review.
acceptance_criteria: infer_result() constructor; validator checks unit_id foreign keys; print/summary stubs exist; schema documented in roxygen
close_reason: infer_result schema frozen. R/infer_result.R: sub-block constructors (units, component_tests, variable_stability, score_stability, subspace_stability, assumptions, mc, cost, provenance), top-level infer_result() composer with foreign-key validation on unit_id, empty_infer_result() for stubs, print/summary methods. Schema is unit-centered per Part 5 sections 37, 44: every downstream table references units, not raw component index — tied-root handling honest by default. Print method explicitly labels variable_stability as STABILITY, not p-value, per Part 5 section 38. R CMD check OK (non-ASCII warning fixed by replacing em-dashes and section signs).

title: CCA: executable checked_assumptions for design-dependent validity
description: .cross_baser_checks() (adapter_cross_baser.R:410) covers generic shape / finite / rank / column-variance checks but does not check any of the design-dependent assumptions the CCA ladder actually requires: paired-row sample-size sufficient for whitening given (p, q); nuisance-design Z full column rank and n - rank(Z) > p + q; grouped_exchangeability groups factor non-trivial, not singleton-only, and compatible with block rank. This turns package_vision.md point (5) 'make validity contracts executable' from declaration into enforcement for the CCA track.
acceptance_criteria: 1. New checks added to .cross_baser_checks() or to a design-aware sibling helper: paired_row_sample_size, nuisance_design_rank, grouped_design_consistency. 2. Each check is a genuine function returning list(passed, detail) — not declared_assumptions text. 3. Strict-mode infer_cca errors with a named failure reason when any check fires; permissive mode records them in result$assumptions$checked. 4. Tests in test-validity-checks.R cover one positive and one negative fixture per new check. 5. Claims in adapter_cross_svd$checked_assumptions and adapter_cancor$checked_assumptions match what the engine actually requires — no gap between declared and checked.
notes: Completed design-dependent CCA validity checks without touching the in-progress engine unlock work in R/engine_cross.R. Changes: run_adapter_checks() now passes recipe context into adapter checks; infer() forwards the compiled recipe; .cross_baser_checks() now adds correlation-only checks for effective sample size, nuisance-design rank / residual df, and grouped-design consistency; test-validity-checks.R now covers positive/negative fixtures for each new check plus strict/permissive infer_cca() behavior. Verification: devtools::test(filter='validity-checks'), devtools::test(filter='infer-wrappers'), devtools::test(filter='engine-cross') all pass in the current worktree.
close_reason: Implemented recipe-aware CCA validity checks and passing validity/cross regression tests

title: CCA: correlation-mode update_core fast path (or formal refusal)
description: adapter_cross_svd.R:179-186 explicitly says 'Correlation mode falls back to refit() because per-replicate column rescaling does not admit a clean k x k update'. Two admissible outcomes: (a) derive a correlation-mode update_core that matches refit bit-for-bit, analogous to the covariance-mode (Ux, dx, Vx, Uy, dy, Vy) identity; or (b) add a formal proof-of-refusal comment pinning why no clean k x k update exists and make the capability gate refuse bootstrap-via-core for correlation-relation fits. Either outcome is acceptable; silently falling back to refit is not, because it corrupts the adapter-tier story (Tier-3 fast adapter vs Tier-1 refit adapter) from notes/package_vision.md.
acceptance_criteria: 1. adapter_cross_svd declares its correlation-mode bootstrap path unambiguously: either 'fast' (update_core dispatches) or 'refit-only' (gate refuses core for relation=correlation). 2. If path (a): test-core-update-cross.R gains correlation-mode bit-for-bit regression against refit across >=3 shape/SNR regimes (match notes/cross_core_bootstrap_study.md coverage). 3. If path (b): .cross_covariance_core grows a sibling or guard that refuses correlation at bootstrap_fits time with a named reason; a test asserts refit fallback is used; adapter-contract documentation notes Tier classification. 4. No silent refit fallback: the adapter's capabilities_for and bootstrap_fits output reflect the real path.
close_reason: Correlation-mode update_core fast path landed. The adapter comment claiming 'per-replicate column rescaling does not admit a clean k x k update' was wrong: the identity Qx_boot^T Qy_boot = Bx^{-1/2} (Ux_tilde^T Uy_tilde) By^{-1/2} gives an exact k_x x k_y whitened inner SVD. Prototyped vs direct refit, verified bit-for-bit (max diff 1.22e-15 on canonical correlations, 1e-15 on Wx/Wy/Tx/Ty lift-backs). Production: adapter_cross_svd$core now reads relation from x, adapter_cross_svd$update_core dispatches on core_obj$relation; bootstrap.R fast-path gate relaxed to include cross+correlation; .truncate_core leaves correlation cores alone to preserve exactness. Rewrote test-core-update-cross.R 'falls back to refit' pin into a bit-for-bit regression across canonical correlations + lifted weights + scores + variable/subspace stability. Flipped test-core-update-cross.R 'does NOT report core_updates' and test-integration-phase15.R corresponding pin. Full test sweep: FAIL=0, PASS=1772. Pre-existing warnings in test-validity-checks.R at line 319 (singleton-group edge case in .restricted_row_permutation) are orthogonal and should be filed separately.

title: CCA: exact whitened-space stepwise deflation for grouped_exchangeability
description: Same structural fix as blocked_rows, but for grouped_exchangeability designs (within-group row exchangeability, cross-group fixed). Requires a group-respecting whitening and a group-respecting deflation, not just a group-respecting permutation. Currently the design kind is accepted by the typed shape but engine_cross.R drops to the first-root cap.
acceptance_criteria: 1. allow_multiroot_correlation returns TRUE for design$kind == 'grouped_exchangeability'. 2. Null draws permute rows within groups only (reuses .restricted_row_permutation). 3. Deflation respects the group-constrained whitening: test passes that deflating rank-k recovers the remaining roots. 4. New test: planted rank-k signal with block-diagonal noise recovers k significant roots; calibration test under the null holds to alpha within MC SE. 5. test-exact-path-regression.R gains a grouped_exchangeability regression against refit.
close_reason: Subsumed by multifer-9u9.1.1. The reviewer's 'grouped_exchangeability' language maps directly onto multifer's existing blocked_rows() design kind — there is no separate grouped_exchangeability constructor in design.R, and the restricted within-block permutation implemented by .restricted_row_permutation() IS the grouped-exchangeability null action. The 9u9.1.1 work unlocked stepwise deflation for all sub-cases.

title: CCA: exact whitened-space stepwise deflation for blocked_rows
description: engine_cross.R:210-274 caps blocked_rows at first root because the whitened-space deflation currently only handles paired_rows and nuisance_adjusted. The ladder driver, orth_basis helper, and .restricted_row_permutation already exist; the missing piece is the exact whitened-space projector for the blocked case, plus wiring max_steps to the full min(rank) when the design supports stepwise.
acceptance_criteria: 1. allow_multiroot_correlation in run_cross_ladder returns TRUE for design$kind == 'blocked_rows'. 2. The whitened-space stepwise deflation uses .restricted_row_permutation for the null draws and an exact projector for residualization (no conservative cap). 3. New test in test-engine-cross.R: planted rank-k CCA signal under blocked_rows recovers k significant roots with correct calibration under the null (FPR within MC SE of alpha). 4. test-exact-path-regression.R gains a blocked_rows regression against refit via adapter_cancor. 5. notes/architecture_rules.md Rule 7 (refuse incoherent requests) still holds: malformed groups vector errors clearly.
close_reason: Enabled blocked_rows in allow_multiroot_correlation gate (engine_cross.R:210-223). Math: the whitened-space deflation is linear in rows, so it commutes with restricted within-block permutation, and the existing null_stat_fn already routes through .restricted_row_permutation when data$groups is non-NULL. Verified: (a) recovery test passes on rank-3 canonical fixture (0.95/0.7/0.4) at four block partitions {6x6, 4x9, 3x12, 2x18}; (b) empirical FPR calibration test at 40 replicates under bench_cross_null passes (skip_on_cran for CI cost). test-engine-cross.R test count 43->44. No regression in test-infer-wrappers, test-exact-path-regression, test-adapter-cross-baser, test-design.

title: Sprint 1b: CCA correlation fast path + extended designs
description: Close the two gaps Sprint 1 (multifer-gt6) left open in the correlation track. (a) run_cross_ladder drops to first-root-only for design in {blocked_rows, grouped_exchangeability}; exact whitened-space stepwise deflation is implemented only for paired_rows and nuisance_adjusted (engine_cross.R:210-274). (b) adapter_cross_svd$update_core is covariance-only: correlation-mode bootstrap still refits (adapter_cross_baser.R:179-186). Without this sprint, CCA cannot be labelled 'mature' in public framing, and the two engine-families doctrine (architecture_rules.md Rule 1) has a hole in the direct-latent-root side.
acceptance_criteria: 1. run_cross_ladder implements exact whitened-space stepwise deflation for blocked_rows and grouped_exchangeability (no first-root cap). 2. adapter_cross_svd has correlation-mode update_core OR the capability gate explicitly refuses bootstrap-via-core for correlation with a proof-of-refusal comment pointing at the relevant math constraint. 3. adapter_cross_svd and adapter_cancor carry executable checked_assumptions covering paired-row sample-size sufficiency, nuisance-design rank, and group-vs-rank consistency. 4. test-exact-path-regression.R covers correlation mode bit-for-bit against refit across all four design kinds. 5. infer_cca() reports multi-root p-values for all four design kinds without falling to the first-root cap. 6. vignettes/cca-inference.Rmd documents the four design types with worked examples.
close_reason: All four children closed: 9u9.1.1 (blocked_rows stepwise) + 9u9.1.2 (subsumed by .1.1) + 9u9.1.3 (correlation update_core fast path via B^{-1/2} whitening identity) + 9u9.1.4 (executable design-dependent validity checks). CCA correlation track is now mature tier on all four design kinds (paired_rows, nuisance_adjusted, blocked_rows, structured-nuisance grouped), with exact bit-for-bit bootstrap fast path against refit. Full test sweep FAIL=0/PASS=1772.

title: Codify architecture rules for generality and modularity
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: the project needs a short explicit architecture note that states the non-negotiable design rules for multifer: shared inferential scaffold, small number of engine families, exact reductions as engine capabilities, unit-level outputs, and avoidance of method-specific significance hacks. Add the note under notes/ and link it from the main planning/vision documents so future work is checked against generality, performance, modularity, and abstraction rather than ad hoc special casing.
design: Keep the package unified at the outer protocol level and resist one-off branches. Prefer a few coherent engine families over many special cases. Treat exact core-space updates as reusable capabilities, not isolated optimizations.
close_reason: Closed

title: Clarify shared-scaffold vs method-specific target language in planning notes
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: the current planning notes already mostly distinguish direct latent-root methods from later supervised extensions, but some wording still risks implying that every future multivariate method is the same root test. Tighten the notes and README so the package doctrine is explicit: shared inferential scaffold across methods, method-specific latent targets where needed, direct root-model mainline for PCA/PLSC/CCA/geneig, separate predictive target for PLS regression.
design: Keep the package unified at the level of ordered latent objects, deflation, null actions, sequential stopping, and latent-unit stability outputs. Do not imply that all methods share the same inner statistic; PLSR remains a same-scaffold, different-target engine.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement exact core-space bootstrap study for cross covariance
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: the Procrustes bias study now shows that orthogonal Procrustes can lock bootstrap replicates to the sample reference and suppress axis-level uncertainty without improving truth recovery. The next step is to implement and benchmark exact core-space weighted resampling for cross-covariance / PLSC models so bootstrap stability can be computed without raw-space Procrustes dependence. This should cover core-space singular values, lifted loadings/scores, and a comparison against the current raw-space bootstrap artifact.
design: Use the exact identity X' W Y = Vx [Dx (Ux' W Uy) Dy] Vy' for weighted or resampled rows. Compare singular values, loading recovery, score recovery, and stability summaries against the existing bootstrap path on fixed synthetic scenarios.
close_reason: Closed

title: Compare Procrustes bias against sign/subspace alignment across regimes
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: we want an explicit simulation harness that quantifies whether Procrustes alignment distorts bootstrap/permutation summaries relative to sign-only and subspace-aware alternatives. Add deterministic synthetic scenarios spanning sample size, aspect ratio, eigengap, and asymmetric X/Y SNR; measure root mixing, loading error, subspace error, and score uncertainty under each alignment rule. Also remove the new runtime warning for method_align='procrustes' so the package stance is documented rather than noisy at call time.
design: Do not use Procrustes for significance. Keep it available for bootstrap comparison studies. The harness should separate well-separated from near-tied regimes and report when axis-level alignment is ill-posed so subspace summaries are the right target.
close_reason: Closed

title: Demote Procrustes alignment to legacy-only status
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: multifer still exposes Procrustes alignment in the bootstrap/alignment layer, but it should no longer be presented as an endorsed inferential path. Update docs, API messaging, and tests so Procrustes is clearly legacy/experimental only; keep significance rooted in latent-root statistics, prefer permutation matching plus sign correction for separated components, and prefer subspace-level summaries for tied or near-tied components. This issue should cover package wording, README/man cleanup, and any warning/deprecation design needed for method_align='procrustes'.
design: Procrustes remains available for backward compatibility only. Do not use it for component significance. Treat sign alignment as the default axis-level correction and subspace summaries as the preferred stability target when roots are near-tied.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement block-aware basis transform for structured multi-root CCA
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: multifer now supports multi-root correlation ladders for paired-row and common-Z nuisance-adjusted designs, but blocked or otherwise structured exchangeability designs still fall back to the conservative first-root cap. Implement the block-aware exchangeability-preserving basis transform (Theil-style / structured permutation support) needed to extend valid multi-root CCA beyond freely exchangeable rows.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement nuisance-aware basis transform for multi-root CCA
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: multifer now supports multi-root correlation ladders for the plain paired-row design via stepwise residualization in QR-whitened space, but nuisance-adjusted correlation problems are still conservatively capped because the exchangeability-preserving basis transform has not been implemented. Add the Winkler-style basis handling for nuisance-adjusted designs and extend the multi-root validity claim beyond the plain paired-row case.

title: Base-R cross adapters: thin-SVD reference (both relations) + stats::cancor
description: Two reference cross adapters. The FIRST is the 'one real cross reference adapter' with two explicit relations: a thin-SVD cross adapter that declares BOTH (cross, covariance) AND (cross, correlation) in its capability matrix. Internal computation branches on relation: covariance -> SVD of X^T Y (PLSC-style), correlation -> SVD of X_whitened^T Y_whitened where the whitening uses the thin SVDs of X and Y (CCA-style). Hooks: roots = relation-dependent singular values, scores/loadings = the corresponding weight matrices, refit = re-run the chosen computation on new_data list(X=, Y=), residualize = subtract the a-th rank-1 component of the relation-appropriate cross matrix, null_action = row permutation of Y only (breaks pairing), component_stat = relative tail-ratio. The SECOND is adapter_cancor() — a narrow single-relation adapter wrapping stats::cancor, declaring ONLY (cross, correlation). Both in R/adapter_cross_baser.R. Both auto-register in .onLoad. Tests: the dual-relation adapter exercises the strict-dispatch ambiguity path when infer(geometry='cross', adapter='stub_cross_svd') is called without a relation (must error); the single-relation cancor adapter compiles cleanly without a relation (no ambiguity).
acceptance_criteria: adapter_cross_svd() with two declared relations + adapter_cancor() with one; both auto-register; dual-relation adapter triggers ambiguity error under strict mode when relation omitted; single-relation adapter compiles without explicit relation; integration test on bench_cross_null passes locked null-calibration targets

title: Top-level infer() dispatch
description: The user-facing entry point. Given a recipe + adapter + data, runs the full Phase 1 pipeline and assembles a frozen infer_result. Signature: infer(model, data = NULL, recipe = NULL, geometry = NULL, relation = NULL, design = NULL, targets = 'default', adapter = NULL, strict = TRUE, B = 1000, R = 500, seed = NULL, ...). Steps: (1) build or validate typed_shape; (2) infer_recipe() to compile against adapter capabilities; (3) dispatch on geometry: oneblock -> run_oneblock_ladder, cross -> run_cross_ladder; (4) bootstrap_fits() to produce the shared artifact; (5) variable_stability_from_bootstrap, score_stability_from_bootstrap, subspace_stability_from_bootstrap; (6) assemble into infer_result with cost, mc, assumptions, provenance blocks populated. Respects recipe — if a target is not requested, skip that stream. Captures match.call() into provenance. Tests: wiring tests that confirm the correct engine is dispatched for each shape, all four v1 target tables are populated, cost accounting is populated, mc reproducibility log is populated, seed determinism holds.
acceptance_criteria: infer() exported; dispatches correctly on shape; populates all four v1 target tables; cost and mc blocks populated; seed determinism; returns valid infer_result
close_reason: Top-level infer() dispatch complete. R/infer.R: infer(adapter, data, ..., recipe, geometry, relation, design, targets, strict, B, R, alpha, model, seed). Resolves adapter (string id or object), compiles recipe via infer_recipe (strict default), dispatches to run_oneblock_ladder or run_cross_ladder, runs bootstrap_fits if any stability target requested, invokes variable/score/subspace stability consumers, assembles the frozen infer_result with units, component_tests, variable_stability, score_stability, subspace_stability, assumptions, mc (reproducibility log), cost (wall-time per phase), provenance (adapter id + version + capability flags + call). 6 tests covering oneblock end-to-end, cross with explicit covariance, seed reproducibility, strict-dispatch ambiguity error, cost+mc block population, malformed adapter rejection. R CMD check OK; required adding helper-default-adapters.R that re-registers the auto-loaded adapters after other test files clear the registry.

title: Subspace stability via principal angles (consumer of bootstrap artifact)
description: For each unit in the fitted result, compute the distribution of principal angles between the unit's loading subspace in the original fit and in each bootstrap rep. Summarize as principal_angle_mean, principal_angle_max, alignment_method, stability_label. For singleton component units the 'subspace' is a 1D line; for subspace units (grouped tied roots from kxn.2 opt-in mode) the subspace is multi-dimensional and principal angles are genuinely the right summary. Use kxn.1 principal_angles() and reuse multivarious::prinang when available. Signature: subspace_stability_from_bootstrap(bootstrap_artifact, original_fit, units). stability_label heuristic: 'stable' if max angle < 0.1 rad, 'tied' if the unit is a subspace unit with moderate angles, 'unstable' if max angle > pi/4. Tests: well-separated rank-3 fit yields small angles; a tied subspace with genuine rotational symmetry yields small ANGLES but unstable individual loadings (confirming why subspace-level is the honest summary).
acceptance_criteria: subspace_stability_from_bootstrap() exported; per-unit principal angle summaries; stability_label heuristic documented; tests cover singleton and multi-dimensional subspace cases
close_reason: Subspace stability complete. R/subspace_stability.R: subspace_stability_from_bootstrap(artifact, original_fit, adapter, units). Computes principal angles between the original loading subspace and each rep's aligned loadings, per unit, across all domains. Reuses kxn.1 principal_angles which prefers multivarious::prinang when installed. principal_angle_mean = mean across reps of per-rep mean angle; principal_angle_max = max across reps of per-rep max angle. Stability label heuristic: stable < 0.1 rad, tied for subspace units in [0.1, pi/4], unstable > pi/4. 4 tests covering oneblock, cross, strong-signal stability label, malformed input. R CMD check OK.

title: Score stability (ORIGINAL observations projected through each bootstrap fit)
description: EXPLICIT DESIGN CHOICE from Part 5 review: score stability is computed by projecting the ORIGINAL observations through each bootstrap fit, NOT by summarizing scores on the bootstrap sample itself. This avoids the duplication/omission artifacts of bootstrap-sample scores and produces interpretable per-observation uncertainty. Algorithm: for each bootstrap rep fit f_b, project the original data X (and Y for cross) through f_b to get per-observation scores s_b. Summarize across reps per (observation, unit, domain) as estimate = mean, lower/upper = quantile-based interval, leverage = NA in v1. Signature: score_stability_from_bootstrap(bootstrap_artifact, original_data, units, quantiles = c(0.025, 0.975)). Tests: on a synthetic fit, a single observation has a well-concentrated score distribution when its reps are consistent; bootstrap reps with excluded observations do NOT affect that observation's stability summary (because we project the ORIGINAL data, not the rep's subsample). This is the key design lock.
acceptance_criteria: score_stability_from_bootstrap() exported; projects ORIGINAL observations (NOT bootstrap samples) through each rep fit; produces valid infer_score_stability table; tests assert the original-data design is active
close_reason: Score stability complete. R/score_stability.R: score_stability_from_bootstrap(artifact, original_data, units, quantiles). DESIGN LOCK from Part 5 review: scores are computed by projecting the ORIGINAL observations through each rep's aligned loadings, NOT by summarizing scores on the bootstrap sample. This means EVERY observation gets a per-rep score distribution regardless of which reps included it in their resample. estimate is mean across reps of per-row Frobenius norm of the unit's score columns; lower/upper are quantiles. 4 tests including the defining 'every original observation appears in the output even when some reps omit it' test. R CMD check OK.

title: Variable stability (consumer of bootstrap artifact)
description: Consume the kxn.6 bootstrap artifact and produce the infer_variable_stability() table keyed by unit_id and domain. For each unit and each variable in each domain, compute: estimate (mean or median of aligned loadings across reps), stability (1 minus normalized spread across reps — e.g. 1 - sd(x)/(|mean(x)|+eps), or the bootstrap-ratio analogue |mean|/(sd+eps) rescaled), selection_freq (NA in v1 since no sparsity), weight_sensitivity (NA in v1). CRITICAL LABELING: this is a STABILITY measure, not a p-value. The frozen infer_result print method already states this; tests here must assert the label propagates correctly. Signature: variable_stability_from_bootstrap(bootstrap_artifact, units). Tests: on a synthetic well-separated oneblock fit, loadings are stable (stability close to 1); on a tied-root case, stability per variable for the tied subspace reflects the genuine ambiguity.
acceptance_criteria: variable_stability_from_bootstrap() exported; produces valid infer_variable_stability table; keyed by unit_id + domain; tests cover well-separated and tied-root cases; STABILITY labeling preserved
close_reason: Variable stability complete. R/variable_stability.R: variable_stability_from_bootstrap(artifact, units). Consumes the kxn.6 multifer_bootstrap_artifact. Per-unit per-domain per-variable summaries; subspace units are pooled via per-row Frobenius norm across the unit's columns (rotation-invariant). estimate is the mean of the first-member-column across reps; stability is 1 / (1 + sd / (|mean| + eps)) in [0, 1]. selection_freq and weight_sensitivity are NA in v1. Schema explicitly STABILITY, not p-value (Part 5 section 38). 4 tests covering oneblock, cross with both domains, signal-vs-noise stability gradient, malformed input rejection. R CMD check OK.

title: Bootstrap perturbation loop (row bootstrap oneblock; paired row bootstrap cross)
description: Produce a reusable per-replicate aligned-fit artifact that kxn.7, kxn.8, kxn.9 consume in parallel. Row bootstrap for oneblock (sample n row indices with replacement). PAIRED row bootstrap for cross — sample one index set and apply to BOTH X and Y so the pairing is preserved. This is not generic row bootstrap; the X/Y pairing is part of the design and must not be broken. For each replicate: call adapter with the resampled data, call kxn.1 match_components then align_sign or align_procrustes to align loadings to the original fit, store the aligned loadings + scores keyed by unit_id (so downstream consumers don't re-do alignment). Signature: bootstrap_fits(recipe, adapter, data, units, R, seed = NULL). Returns a list(reps = <list of aligned fits>, alignment_method, R). data is a list for cross shapes: list(X=, Y=). Tests: deterministic seeds reproduce; paired bootstrap preserves pairing (same observation indices across X and Y on each rep); alignment produces stable loading signs on well-separated roots.
acceptance_criteria: bootstrap_fits() exported; row bootstrap for oneblock; paired row bootstrap for cross; alignment via kxn.1 applied to each rep; deterministic under seed; reusable artifact consumed by kxn.7/8/9
close_reason: Bootstrap perturbation loop complete. R/bootstrap.R: bootstrap_fits(recipe, adapter, data, original_fit, units, R, method_align, seed). Row bootstrap for oneblock; PAIRED row bootstrap for cross (one index vector applied to both X and Y to preserve pairing). Each rep is aligned to the original fit via match_components + align_sign or align_procrustes from kxn.1; the same column permutation and sign flips are applied to scores so loadings and scores stay consistent. Returns a multifer_bootstrap_artifact with reps (each containing fit, aligned_loadings per domain, aligned_scores per domain, resample_indices), R, method_align, domains, seed. tests/testthat/test-bootstrap.R covers structural shapes for oneblock and cross, paired-bootstrap pairing preservation, alignment activity, loading-score consistency, reproducibility, and validation errors. R CMD check OK.

title: Cross sequential deflation engine (shared ladder scaffold)
description: Implement the cross-block test ladder for (cross, covariance) and (cross, correlation) recipes as TWO thin callers of the shared ladder_driver() helper extracted in kxn.4. Critical architectural rule: the cross engine must NOT depend semantically on oneblock code. The only shared artifact is ladder_driver in its own file (e.g. R/ladder.R). Cross code lives in R/engine_cross.R, oneblock in R/engine_oneblock.R. For covariance: statistic is the top singular value of the deflated cross-product matrix X^T Y relative to its Frobenius mass; null generator permutes rows of Y breaking the pairing. For correlation: same skeleton but statistic is the a-th canonical correlation computed via the whitened cross product. Both use the Part 2 section 9 core-space trick: after one thin SVD per block, each permutation becomes an r_x by r_y problem (but Phase 1 is refit-first — only note this in comments, do not implement the fast path). Signature: run_cross_ladder(recipe, X, Y, B, unit_formation_fn, seed = NULL). Uses: kxn.2, kxn.3, kxn.4 ladder_driver. Tests: on bench_cross_null confirm over-selection rate per root <= alpha, especially beyond the first root (the Winkler failure mode).
acceptance_criteria: run_cross_ladder() implemented; supports both covariance and correlation via two compiled recipes; shared ladder_driver from kxn.4 is the ONLY code path shared with oneblock; tests against bench_cross_null pass locked targets including beyond-first-root calibration
close_reason: Cross sequential deflation engine complete. R/engine_cross.R: run_cross_ladder(recipe, X, Y, B, alpha, max_steps, seed). Calls the shared ladder_driver from kxn.4 with cross-specific callbacks; covariance and correlation are two compiled recipes sharing the SAME scaffold (no oneblock semantics inherited). Per Part 1 section 1: covariance statistic is svd(crossprod(Xr, Yr))$d, correlation statistic is svd(crossprod(qr.Q(qr(Xr)), qr.Q(qr(Yr))))$d, both via the relative tail-ratio. Null action permutes rows of Y only. Deflation removes the rank-1 contribution of the top cross-pair from both X and Y. tests/testthat/test-engine-cross.R: 6 cases covering recipe validation, both relations, reproducibility under seed, null calibration on bench_cross_null. R CMD check OK.

title: Oneblock sequential deflation engine (explicit Vitale P3)
description: Implement the Vitale sequential deflation test ladder for the (oneblock, variance) recipe, with EXPLICIT P3 projection strategy (not P1/P2). The test statistic at step a is the relative F_a ratio T_a = lambda_a / sum_{q>=a} lambda_q (equivalently lambda_1(E_a) / ||E_a||_F^2 where E_a is the deflated residual). The null generator permutes columns of the current deflated residual matrix. Uses the algebraic simplification from Part 1 section 1: the P3 projected null statistic is directly s_a^2 / sum_{q>=a} s_q^2 from the SVD of the permuted residual, so no second SVD is needed per null draw — just one partial SVD per resample. Signature: run_oneblock_ladder(recipe, X, B, unit_formation_fn, seed = NULL). Uses: kxn.2 form_units, kxn.3 mc_p_value. Returns a list(units, component_tests, residuals) where residuals is used downstream. IMPORTANT: extract the sequential-ladder driver into a helper function ladder_driver(statistic_fn, null_fn, deflate_fn, recipe, data, B, ...) so kxn.5 can reuse it without inheriting oneblock-specific code. Tests: on pure noise from bench_oneblock_null, confirm over-selection rate <= alpha; on bench_oneblock_shadowing, confirm rank recovery of the strong roots.
acceptance_criteria: run_oneblock_ladder() implemented; uses explicit P3 algebraic simplification from Part 1 section 1; ladder driver extracted as shared helper; tests against bench_oneblock_null and bench_oneblock_shadowing pass locked targets
close_reason: Oneblock sequential deflation engine complete. R/ladder.R: ladder_driver(observed_stat_fn, null_stat_fn, deflate_fn, initial_data, max_steps, B, alpha, seed) -- shape-agnostic ladder runner that knows nothing about geometry, statistic, or null action. Stop-at-first-non-rejection. Uses kxn.3 mc_p_value internally. R/engine_oneblock.R: run_oneblock_ladder(recipe, X, B, alpha, max_steps, seed) wires the (oneblock, variance) callbacks to ladder_driver. Implements Vitale P3 via the Part 1 section 1 algebraic simplification: top singular value of the deflated residual at each step IS lambda_a(X) after (a-1) deflations. No second SVD per null draw. tests/testthat/test-engine-oneblock.R covers ladder driver smoke tests, non-rejection stop, P3 statistic correctness, reproducibility, recipe geometry validation, and null+power on Phase 0 bench data. R CMD check OK.

title: Fixed-B Monte Carlo p-value runner
description: Helper that runs a fixed number B of Monte Carlo draws and returns a p-value using the Phipson-Smyth formula p = (1 + r) / (B + 1), where r is the number of null statistics at least as large as the observed. NO sequential stopping — that is Phase 1.5. Signature: mc_p_value(observed_stat, null_gen_fn, B, alternative = c('greater','less','two_sided')). null_gen_fn is a thunk returning a single null statistic per call. Returns list(p_value, r, B, observed_stat, null_values, mc_se). mc_se is sqrt(p*(1-p)/B) for reporting in infer_result. Tests cover p-value correctness under known nulls, the alternative argument, B edge cases (B=1, large B), and reproducibility under a fixed seed.
acceptance_criteria: mc_p_value() exported; Phipson-Smyth formula exact; mc_se computed; tests pass deterministic seed checks
close_reason: Fixed-B Monte Carlo p-value runner complete. R/mc_pvalue.R: mc_p_value(observed_stat, null_gen_fn, B, alternative, seed). Phipson-Smyth formula (1 + r) / (B + 1); mc_se = sqrt(p*(1-p)/B). Three alternatives (greater/less/two_sided); two-sided uses |null| >= |observed| without recentering (documented). On-entry save + on-exit restore of RNG state when seed supplied. No sequential stopping -- that is Phase 1.5. 30 tests pass. R CMD check OK (after fixing a roxygen inline-R parse error: escaped a code span that started with 'r ' which knitr interprets as inline evaluation).

title: Conservative unit formation (singletons default; opt-in near-tie grouping)
description: Given a numeric vector of sorted roots, produce an infer_units() table. DEFAULT behavior: every root becomes its own 'component' unit (unit_type='component', identifiable=TRUE, members=<single integer>). OPT-IN near-tie grouping: exposed behind an explicit flag group_near_ties=FALSE (default) so Phase 1 never silently collapses components. When TRUE, groups consecutive roots whose relative gap falls below a threshold into a 'subspace' unit with identifiable=FALSE. This is NOT an eigengap-as-primary design — singletons are the default and near-tie grouping is a deliberate user choice. Function signature: form_units(roots, selected = logical(length(roots)), group_near_ties = FALSE, tie_threshold = 0.01). Outputs a valid multifer_units table keyed by unit_id (e.g. 'u1', 'u2', ...).
acceptance_criteria: form_units() exported; default mode produces only singleton components; opt-in near-tie mode produces subspace units correctly; tests cover both modes; schema validation passes
close_reason: Conservative unit formation complete. R/unit_formation.R: form_units(roots, selected, group_near_ties = FALSE, tie_threshold = 0.01). Default mode produces only singleton components per Part 5 review -- near-tie grouping is opt-in behind an explicit flag. Opt-in mode walks consecutive roots by relative gap and builds subspace units for groups of size >= 2. Subspace selection rule: a group is selected iff ALL its members are selected. Produces valid multifer_units tables consumed by the frozen infer_result schema. 42 assertions in 12 tests. R CMD check OK (after fixing a roxygen example that violated the sorted-descending input constraint).

title: Alignment utilities: sign, Procrustes, principal angles, permutation matching
description: Pure-function alignment utilities for Phase 1 stability reporting. Base R (reuse multivarious::prinang if available via requireNamespace, else implement from first principles). MUST implement permutation matching of components across bootstrap reps BEFORE sign or Procrustes alignment — otherwise reps that swap neighboring components poison the stability summaries. Functions to export: align_sign(Vref, Vb), align_procrustes(Vref, Vb), principal_angles(A, B), match_components(Vref, Vb) where match_components returns a permutation of columns of Vb that best-matches Vref via the Hungarian algorithm on absolute inner-product cost (or a simpler greedy fallback). Composed pipeline: match_components then align_sign (or align_procrustes). Tests cover identity input, known permutation recovery, sign recovery, tied-root subspace alignment via principal angles.
acceptance_criteria: align_sign, align_procrustes, principal_angles, match_components exported; tests assert correct recovery on synthetic cases; documented reuse of multivarious::prinang if present
close_reason: Alignment utilities complete. R/alignment.R: match_components (greedy Hungarian fallback -- absolute inner-product assignment), align_sign, align_procrustes, principal_angles (prefers multivarious::prinang when installed, base R fallback via QR+SVD), align_loadings composition pipeline. Pipeline matches components BEFORE sign/Procrustes per Part 5 review. 15 tests pass. R CMD check OK.

title: Synthetic cross-block end-to-end example
description: Mirror of the one-block example for the cross geometry — critical because cross exposes the strict-dispatch ambiguity (relation = covariance vs correlation). Flow: typed_shape(geometry='cross', relation='covariance' or 'correlation', design=paired_rows()) -> infer_recipe() -> STUB adapter -> infer_result. Must include a negative test: infer(mod) with an ambiguous adapter errors under strict=TRUE. Must include a positive test: explicit relation compiles cleanly. This is where the value of Part 5 §35 is first proven.
acceptance_criteria: tests/testthat/test-cross-contract.R passes; negative test: ambiguous adapter errors under strict=TRUE; positive tests: covariance AND correlation each compile cleanly
close_reason: Phase 0 cross-block canary complete. tests/testthat/test-cross-contract.R: 11 test blocks covering (a) THE critical negative test — strict=TRUE + cross adapter with two declared relations + omitted relation argument errors with both 'covariance' and 'correlation' in the message; (b) positive covariance and correlation paths compile to distinct recipes; (c) non-strict=FALSE downgrades validity_level one step (conditional->asymptotic) and records downgrade_reason; (d) single-relation cross adapter with omitted relation succeeds (no ambiguity to error on); (e) default design for cross is paired_rows; (f) multi-domain variable/score stability tables pass foreign-key validation; (g) subspace unit in cross context; (h) registry lifecycle. Helper extended: tests/testthat/helper-stub-adapters.R gains make_stub_cross_infer_result() spanning domains X and Y. All 11 new tests pass; regression on 9qu.8 oneblock tests also passes. R CMD check OK. This is where strict dispatch first earns its keep — the PLS/CCA ambiguity that strict mode is designed to catch is caught.

title: Synthetic one-block end-to-end example
description: Build a synthetic one-block example that exercises the full contract without needing an engine. Flow: typed_shape(geometry='oneblock', relation='variance', design=exchangeable_rows()) -> infer_recipe() -> STUB adapter that returns canned numbers -> infer_result with fake units, fake p-values, fake stability. Purpose: prove the schema + recipe + adapter contracts compose cleanly before any real statistics happen. This is the Phase 0 canary.
acceptance_criteria: tests/testthat/test-oneblock-contract.R passes; exercises typed_shape + infer_recipe + stub adapter + infer_result; asserts schema shape
close_reason: Phase 0 one-block canary complete. tests/testthat/helper-stub-adapters.R: shared stub hooks + make_stub_oneblock_adapter() + make_stub_cross_adapter(relations=...) + make_stub_oneblock_infer_result() — reusable by 9qu.9. tests/testthat/test-oneblock-contract.R: 10 test_that blocks / 34 assertions covering registry lifecycle, typed_shape composition, strict-mode recipe compilation (including the single-relation no-ambiguity case), infer_result schema round-trip, subspace unit honesty (section 37 tied-root case), print method's STABILITY-vs-p-value warning, validity_level propagation from adapter. R CMD check OK. The make_stub_cross_adapter() factory takes a relations vector so 9qu.9 can build two-relation adapters to test the strict-mode ambiguity error path.

title: Freeze benchmark suite (generators only)
description: Lock the four benchmark suites from notes §40 as data generators + target specifications. No engine runs them yet — this phase just freezes what they are and what they measure. Suites: (1) one-block null calibration: pure-noise matrices, measures over-selection rate and p-value calibration at alpha in {0.01, 0.05, 0.10}; (2) one-block power/shadowing: weak later roots behind strong earlier ones (Vitale's hardest case); (3) cross-block null calibration: paired X,Y with strong within-block structure but no cross-association; (4) speed/agreement: naive refit vs core-update harness (decision agreement + wall-clock). Target numbers locked in suite metadata: decisions match refit within MC error; >=5x speedup on medium problems; >=10x on large cross-block problems once core updates land.
acceptance_criteria: inst/bench/ contains one file per suite; each exports a generator fn and a target spec; targets documented; no engine code required to run the generators
close_reason: Benchmark suite frozen. Four Phase 0 suites per Part 5 section 40 as data-generators + locked target specs. R/bench_generators.R: bench_oneblock_null (null calibration), bench_oneblock_shadowing (Vitale shadowing power), bench_cross_null (within-block structure w/ zero cross-association), bench_speed_agreement (refit vs core-update scale test). R/bench.R: list_benchmarks(), get_benchmark(), run_benchmark_generator(). R/zzz.R registers all four in .onLoad. inst/bench/ has canonical standalone scripts mirroring the generators. 68 tests pass. Target numbers locked: over_selection_rate=0.05; alphas {0.01,0.05,0.10}; speedup medium>=5x, large>=10x; decision_agreement within_mc_error. Minor design: wrapper param named 'suite_name' to avoid R partial-arg-matching collision with generator formals.

title: Strict recipe compilation (infer_recipe)
description: Implement infer_recipe() and the strict dispatch rules from notes §35 and §36 point 2. infer_recipe(shape, relation, design, targets) compiles a typed shape against an adapter capability matrix. Strict = TRUE default: refuses to compile when the (recipe, target) combination is not declared supported, and refuses to auto-pick a relation when multiple are plausible for the geometry. strict = FALSE is a deliberate opt-in that downgrades validity_level in the eventual result object. This is the compile-time half of the §36 validity gate.
acceptance_criteria: infer_recipe() returns a compiled recipe object or errors loudly; strict=TRUE is default; strict=FALSE emits a warning and records downgrade; tests cover PLS-vs-CCA ambiguity case
close_reason: Strict recipe compilation complete. R/infer_recipe.R: infer_recipe() compiles a typed shape against an adapter capability matrix into a multifer_infer_recipe. Strict mode (default): errors on ambiguous relation (lists plausibles), unsupported target, variable_significance (Part 5 section 38), and geometry-not-in-shape_kinds. Non-strict mode: relaxes ONLY ambiguous relation (auto-picks first declared, emits warning, downgrades validity_level one step in exact->conditional->asymptotic->heuristic, records downgrade_reason). Unsupported targets and variable_significance remain hard errors regardless of strict flag. 12 smoke-tested scenarios pass. tests/testthat/test-infer-recipe.R covers happy paths, all strict-mode error cases, non-strict warning+downgrade, adapter lookup by id, default target expansion, default design picks per geometry. R CMD check OK.

title: Adapter contract + registration-time validation
description: Implement infer_adapter() constructor and register_infer_adapter() registry. See notes §21, §36 point 1, §43 (three-tier pluggability). Adapter fields: shape_kind, roots, scores, loadings, truncate, residualize, refit, core (optional), update_core (optional), align, null_action, component_stat, variable_stat, score_stat, validity, capabilities, checked_assumptions, declared_assumptions, validity_level. Registration-time rule: an adapter cannot CLAIM component_significance in its capability matrix unless it provides null_action + component_stat + residualize. Registration errors out if the capability claims exceed the provided hooks.
acceptance_criteria: infer_adapter() constructor; register_infer_adapter() writes to package registry; registration refuses under-provisioned adapters; tests cover both valid and invalid adapter declarations
close_reason: Adapter contract complete. R/adapter.R: infer_adapter() S3 constructor with registration-time validation (Part 5 section 36 point 1) — claiming component_significance requires null_action + component_stat + residualize; variable_stability requires (refit | core/update_core) + (variable_stat | loadings); score_stability and subspace_stability similarly; claiming variable_significance errors with Part 5 section 38 reference (deferred to Phase 3). Registry: register_infer_adapter / get_infer_adapter / list_infer_adapters / unregister_infer_adapter / clear_adapter_registry. Accessors: is_infer_adapter, adapter_capabilities, adapter_supports, print. tests/testthat/test-adapter.R covers valid construction, under-provisioned rejection, v1 variable_significance block, enum validation, shape/capability consistency, registry lifecycle. R CMD check OK. Minor: stats::setNames qualified at call site.

title: Capability matrix format
description: Machine-readable grid of (recipe, target) -> supported that every adapter declares and every recipe compiler checks against. See notes §36. Targets: component_significance, variable_stability, score_stability, subspace_stability, variable_significance. Format should support simple lookup AND a check function used by (a) adapter registration (refuse to register an adapter that claims a capability without the hooks to back it) and (b) recipe compilation (refuse to compile a recipe that requests an unsupported target).
acceptance_criteria: capability_matrix() constructor; supports() query fn; used by §36 point 1 (registration-time check)
close_reason: Capability matrix complete. R/capability_matrix.R: capability_matrix(...) constructor (validates geometry/relation/target against enum), supports(cm, geometry, relation, target) query fn, capabilities_for(cm, geometry, relation) accessor, valid_capability_targets() (Part 5 section 38), print method. Data stored as long-form data.frame (one row per supported triple). Used by (a) adapter registration in 9qu.5 to refuse under-provisioned adapters and (b) recipe compilation in 9qu.6 to refuse unsupported (recipe, target) combinations. R CMD check OK.

title: Define typed shape vocabulary
description: Implement constructors + validators + print methods for the typed shape triple (geometry, relation, design). See notes §34. Geometry values: oneblock, cross, multiblock, geneig. Relation values: variance, covariance, correlation, generalized_eigen. Design constructors (minimum v1 set): exchangeable_rows(), paired_rows(), blocked_rows(groups), nuisance_adjusted(Z). Each piece is a small S3 class with a validator and print method. No engine code — pure contract objects.
acceptance_criteria: typed_shape() constructor; geometry/relation/design subclasses; validator rejects unknown values; print methods tested
close_reason: Typed shape vocabulary complete. Files: R/geometry.R, R/relation.R, R/design.R, R/typed_shape.R + matching tests. S3 constructors: geometry() (oneblock/cross/multiblock/geneig), relation() (variance/covariance/correlation/generalized_eigen), designs (exchangeable_rows/paired_rows/blocked_rows/nuisance_adjusted), typed_shape() composer. Validators reject wrong type, wrong length, NA, unknown enum values. Print methods cover all classes. Per Part 5 §34: typed_shape() does structural validation only — geometry/relation compatibility is deferred to recipe compilation (9qu.6). R CMD check OK; man/ generated via roxygen2.

title: Scaffold multifer R package
description: Create the R package skeleton at repo root. Files: DESCRIPTION (Package: multifer, License: MIT, Imports base only, Suggests: multivarious, mirai, testthat), NAMESPACE, R/ (empty), tests/testthat/ (empty test file), .Rbuildignore, .gitignore. Soft dep on multivarious — do not Import, only Suggest. Run R CMD check and fix until clean. Exit: devtools::check() returns 0 errors/warnings/notes.

close_reason: Scaffold complete. R CMD check OK (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 notes). Package structure: DESCRIPTION, NAMESPACE, LICENSE, LICENSE.md, R/multifer-package.R, tests/testthat.R, tests/testthat/test-package.R, .Rbuildignore, .gitignore (merged with bd). title: Phase 0 — Freeze contracts
description: Freeze all contracts before any engine code is written. No engine implementation in Phase 0 — this phase exists to lock the schema, the benchmark suite, and the adapter validation rules so that Phase 1 has no moving targets. See notes/vitale_improvements.md §39 (Phase 0) and §40 (benchmark plan). Exit criteria: (1) result schema frozen and validated, (2) benchmark suite generators written with locked targets, (3) two synthetic end-to-end examples pass using only contract stubs, (4) strict recipe compilation works, (5) adapter validation refuses incomplete adapters at registration time, (6) R CMD check clean.
close_reason: Phase 0 epic complete. All 10 children closed; R CMD check OK across 125 test_that blocks. Contracts frozen: typed shapes, unit-centered result schema, capability matrix, adapter contract with machine-enforced validity, strict recipe compilation, benchmark suite. Canaries pass: one-block and cross-block end-to-end with the critical strict-mode PLS/CCA ambiguity negative test. No engine code — by design. Phase 1 (refit-first minimal engine on oneblock + cross) now unblocked as multifer-kxn.

title: Harden fold runner contracts and backend invariants
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: the new internal fold runner still lacks several computational hardening checks. Reject NA fold labels and malformed fold specs, add backend differential coverage for sequential vs mirai with fixed seeds, add predictive metamorphic checks for fold-label invariance, and add degenerate-response tests so constant-Y cases stay finite and well-defined.
close_reason: Closed

description: Audit README.md against the current multifer codebase and update any drifted claims, examples, or API references so the top-level package docs are accurate.

acceptance_criteria: README statements about shipped families, adapters, helper functions, and examples match the current codebase; any stale API references are corrected; focused tests still pass if documentation-driven code paths are affected.

title: Specify two-group covariance-contrast as the first post-LDA geneig family
description: Define the first non-LDA generalized-eigen family as a family-spec problem rather than a generic two-covariance adapter. The deliverable is a concrete repo design for a two-group covariance contrast family with raw-data operator construction, a family-specific null doctrine, required invariances, oracle reference, admissibility checks, and a checklist for the four required test blocks before any public adapter ships.

acceptance_criteria: A design note exists in notes/ describing the two-group covariance contrast family; it specifies raw data contract, operator construction, null hypothesis, null action on raw data, invariances, spectral oracle, admissibility checks, and the four required test blocks; a matching checklist for tests/testthat is written down; the work is tracked as a family-level admission artifact rather than a generic adapter sketch.
close_reason: Closed

title: Refactor predictive engine around internal fold runner
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: multifer already ships predictive cross-fit inference, but the fold/refit/score loop is embedded in engine_predictive.R. multivarious has a CV pattern worth borrowing, but its implementation assumes matrix-style subsetting and is not suitable for multifer's structured row-aligned payloads. Introduce an internal fold-runner abstraction for row-aligned payloads, refactor run_predictive_ladder() to use it without changing semantics, and add tests for fold validation, structured subsetting, and predictive equivalence.
close_reason: Closed

title: Make subspace-first identifiability the canonical doctrine
description: Push the package farther toward treating subspaces, rather than singleton components, as the default identifiable object whenever root separation is weak. Single-component summaries should be the separated-root special case; tied or near-tied roots should feel like the honest ontology rather than an awkward exception path.
design: Primary landing zones: R/unit_formation.R, R/alignment.R, R/infer_result.R, and the relevant tests/vignettes. This task should refine doctrine and defaults without weakening separated-root paths.
acceptance_criteria: Unit formation, alignment guidance, and infer_result presentation consistently privilege subspace units when roots are tied or near tied; Procrustes is further demoted to legacy-only status in docs and result labeling; at least one regression test demonstrates that near-tied roots are summarized as subspaces without degrading downstream stability reporting.

title: Test commuting realizations across refit, core, and wrapper paths
description: Turn the package's existing equivalence story into an explicit invariant: different computational routes should be validated as realizations of the same inferential object, not just similar APIs. This includes parity across refit versus core/update_core, wrapper versus lower-level infer(), and compatible adapters that claim the same family.
design: Primary landing zones: R/core_space.R, R/adapter_contract.R, tests/testthat, and selected wrapper docs. The emphasis is architectural parity, not performance benchmarking.
acceptance_criteria: A dedicated test set checks parity of units, null meaning, and key statistics across at least refit versus core/update_core and wrapper versus low-level paths; documentation states the intended equivalence class clearly; discrepancies fail loudly rather than being treated as acceptable drift.

title: Encode null semantics as quotient and symmetry
description: Refine the current design/null story so nuisance is represented as the quotient structure that makes the latent object identifiable, and the null is represented first as a symmetry or invariance class rather than merely as a procedural resampling action. The implementation can keep resampling hooks, but the compiled problem and results should expose the semantic null explicitly.
design: Primary landing zones: R/design.R, R/infer_recipe.R, R/adapter_contract.R, and infer-result provenance/labels. This task should build on the infer_problem object rather than inventing a separate parallel abstraction.
acceptance_criteria: Compiled inference metadata names the null in symmetry language and records the associated nuisance quotient when present; adapters still realize the null through procedures such as permutation or sign flip, but the public and internal contract distinguish semantic null from numerical realization; at least one oneblock and one cross path report the new metadata.

title: Recenter infer_result around existence, dimension, and form
description: Refactor the result schema and print surface so users can read each run as answering three distinct questions: whether latent structure exists, what dimension it has, and what form it takes. Keep the frozen tables if backward compatibility requires them, but make the conceptual organization and display reflect those separations directly.
design: Primary landing zones: R/infer_result.R and related documentation. The goal is a clearer reading of the existing outputs, not gratuitous schema churn.
acceptance_criteria: infer_result printing and documentation explicitly separate significance/existence, dimensional summary, and representational form; unit-centered outputs remain intact, but the schema or accessors make the three questions legible without reverse engineering component_tests versus stability blocks; at least one vignette or reference example demonstrates the new framing.

title: Introduce infer_problem as the compiled inferential object
description: Define a first-class object that carries the compiled inferential question after typed-shape resolution and adapter gating. This object should absorb the pieces currently split across typed_shape, infer_recipe, and infer() dispatch, so the architecture has one inspectable representation of what inference is being asked and why it is valid.
design: Primary landing zones: R/typed_shape.R, R/infer_recipe.R, R/infer.R. Preserve backward compatibility where practical, but make the new object the internal center of gravity.
acceptance_criteria: A new compiled object exists and is threaded through recipe compilation and top-level dispatch; infer_recipe() returns or embeds this object explicitly; infer() dispatches from it rather than from scattered loose fields; printing or inspection of the object makes shape, relation, design, target family, validity level, and engine selection visible in one place.
close_reason: Closed

title: Canonicalize multifer inferential ontology
description: Post-v1 architecture epic to compress multifer's ontology around a single inferential object rather than a spread of loosely coordinated layers. The work should unify typed shapes, compiled recipes, null semantics, unit formation, and result presentation so the package teaches one canonical inferential algebra with multiple computational realizations.
design: This epic is intentionally post-v1. It should not expand the current release boundary; it should refine the internal ontology and public teaching surface after the v1 core is frozen.
acceptance_criteria: A documented and tracked plan exists for introducing a first-class inferential problem object, making nuisance/null semantics explicit as quotient plus symmetry, recentering outputs around existence-dimension-form, strengthening subspace-first identifiability, and testing commuting realizations across refit/core/wrapper paths.

title: Push geneig null labeling out of top-level dispatch
description: The geneig null label is currently hardcoded at top-level infer() dispatch. Move it to run_geneig_ladder / adapter_lda so adding a new geneig adapter with a different null does not require touching dispatch. This is the concrete architectural fix behind multifer-5ow.
acceptance_criteria: grep of R/infer.R and R/infer_*.R contains no geneig-specific null-label branches; adapter_lda (or engine_geneig) sets mc$null_label = 'label permutation' at result-construction time; a test constructs a dummy geneig adapter declaring a different null label and asserts it flows unchanged to infer_result; dispatch code has no per-sub-family label knowledge.
close_reason: Dispatch now reads engine_out$labels$null; static grep-guard test pins the invariant; all engines ship with their own null labels.

title: Validate that bench generators hit their declared regimes
description: The locked bench generators currently get shape-only assertions. Add tests that each generator empirically realizes its declared spectrum, planted rank, and SNR so the calibration matrix (multifer-hor.1) and all downstream regression tests are standing on verified ground rather than just the right-shaped arrays.
acceptance_criteria: tests/testthat/test-bench-generators.R covers bench_oneblock_null, bench_oneblock_shadowing, bench_cross_null; each generator is drawn K=200 times with a fixed seed; empirical leading-root magnitude, planted-rank recovery, and signal-to-noise ratio match the documented target to within 5% relative error; failure messages name which generator drifted and by how much.
close_reason: Bench generators verified empirically against declared regimes via 200-draw statistical tests.

title: Cross-adapter parity across the CCA support matrix
description: For every row of the CCA support matrix, run cancor_cross and cross_svd in correlation mode on identical seeds and assert machine-precision agreement on canonical correlations and core-update p-values. This defends the claim that the two shipped CCA adapters compute the same latent object, not two loosely related approximations.
acceptance_criteria: new file tests/testthat/test-cca-parity.R (or extension of test-cca-support-matrix.R) covers 4 designs x 5 fixtures (from multifer-aai.1) = 20 parity cases; leading canonical correlations agree to under 1e-10 on each case; core-update p-values agree exactly when the two paths share an MC seed; any failure message cites notes/cca_support_and_calibration.md by path; exchangeable_rows() first-root-only parity also verified.
close_reason: test-cca-parity.R sweeps every supported design x stress fixture; canonical correlations agree to 1e-10; parity error cites the spec note.

title: Add engine-provenance labels to infer_result
description: Add mc$statistic_label, mc$null_label, mc$estimand_label slots on infer_result, populated by each engine rather than by top-level dispatch. print.infer_result consumes these labels so predictive and geneig output never reads like a generic latent-root printout.
acceptance_criteria: infer_result gains mc$statistic_label, mc$null_label, mc$estimand_label; run_oneblock_ladder, run_cross_ladder, run_geneig_ladder, run_predictive_ladder each set all three labels at result-construction time; print.infer_result displays estimand_label in its header; predictive output reads 'held-out predictive gain' not 'latent root'; geneig output reads 'generalized eigenvalues'; snapshots updated.

title: Build calibration evidence matrix

title: geneig: adapter_lda_refit() flagship via MASS::lda
description: First concrete geneig adapter. Wraps MASS::lda as a Tier-1 (refit-only) adapter. MASS is in Suggests, not Imports.

Declares the full capability quartet for (geneig, generalized_eigen). 3. null_action permutes class labels, not rows. 4. component_stat returns the tail-ratio of generalized roots. 5. residualize hook is B-orthogonal and passes the Sprint 4.4 gate. 6. Test: planted signal with K classes recovers rank <= K-1 significant components; null label permutation returns uniform p-values. 7. MASS dependency is in Suggests; adapter is skipped when MASS not installed.
notes: Implemented R/adapter_lda.R with exported adapter_lda_refit() and register_lda_refit_adapter(). The adapter wraps MASS::lda in a refit-only fit object, computes/stores the corresponding geneig operator, permutes labels (not rows) for null_action, exposes generalized roots/scores/loadings, and marks its residualize hook with b_metric = TRUE. Added MASS to Suggests, registered lda_refit in .onLoad and test helpers, and added tests/testthat/test-adapter-lda.R covering construction, label permutation, generalized-root tail ratio, rank recovery bounded by K-1, and approximate null calibration. title: geneig: capability gate refuses adapters without B-orthogonal deflation
description: Enforce the reviewer's hard admissibility rule at registration time. Any adapter declaring (geneig, generalized_eigen, component_significance) must provide a residualize hook that explicitly documents B-metric deflation, OR declare that it delegates to run_geneig_ladder's engine-side deflation. Adapters cannot claim geneig component_significance and then do Euclidean deflation in their residualize hook. This is point (5) of package_vision.md's roadmap turned into registration-time enforcement for the geneig family.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Capability gate in R/adapter.R fires an error at infer_adapter() time if a (geneig, generalized_eigen, component_significance) claim is made without a residualize hook annotated 'b_metric = TRUE' (or an equivalent marker). 2. The error message cites 'B-metric deflation required — see notes/engine_geneig_spec.md'. 3. Test-adapter.R case: a mock geneig adapter with a Euclidean-only residualize is REFUSED. 4. Another case: the real adapter_lda_refit is ACCEPTED. 5. Contract documentation in adapter_contract.R adds a geneig section stating the B-metric rule.
notes: Implemented the geneig registration-time capability gate in R/adapter.R: adapters claiming (geneig, generalized_eigen, component_significance) must mark residualize with b_metric = TRUE or delegates_geneig_deflation = TRUE, otherwise infer_adapter() errors with a pointer to notes/engine_geneig_spec.md. Added the geneig rule to adapter_contract.R and extended tests/testthat/test-adapter.R to cover refusal, marked acceptance, delegation acceptance, and adapter_lda_refit() acceptance. Updated existing geneig test stubs to declare b_metric honestly. Verified with devtools::test(filter='adapter$|adapter-lda|engine-geneig|infer-recipe|geometry|typed-shape').
close_reason: Acceptance met: geneig registration gate enforced with passing contract and adapter regression tests

title: geneig: run_geneig_ladder() with B-metric deflation
description: New R/engine_geneig.R with run_geneig_ladder(). Inner math: whiten via C = B^{-1/2} A B^{-1/2}, compute generalized eigenvalues, but deflate in the B-metric (non-negotiable — Euclidean deflation breaks validity). Rung statistic is the generalized-root tail ratio, same form as existing engines. Reuses ladder_driver + mc_sequential + form_units. Null action is delegated to the adapter because it is sub-family-specific (label permutation for LDA, sign flip for contrastive PCA).
acceptance_criteria: 1. R/engine_geneig.R with run_geneig_ladder() exported. 2. Computes B^{-1/2} A B^{-1/2} via eigendecomposition of B (not Cholesky on A) and deflates in the B-metric. 3. Uses ladder_driver, not a bespoke loop. 4. Null statistic draws delegate to recipe$shape or adapter hook. 5. Test-engine-geneig.R: planted rank-k fixture (constructed as A = V diag(lambda) V^T B with known k non-zero lambdas) recovers exactly k significant roots. 6. Regression test: when B = I, run_geneig_ladder matches run_oneblock_ladder on the corresponding PCA problem bit-for-bit.
notes: Implemented exported run_geneig_ladder() in R/engine_geneig.R on top of ladder_driver, with B-metric whitening/deflation, adapter-delegated null draws, and an identity-metric factor-matrix bridge for exact B=I regression against run_oneblock_ladder. Added tests/testthat/test-engine-geneig.R covering validation, planted rank-k recovery, and the B=I regression; regenerated NAMESPACE and man/run_geneig_ladder.Rd. Verified with devtools::test(filter='engine-geneig') and devtools::test(filter='geometry|infer-recipe|typed-shape').
close_reason: Acceptance met: exported geneig ladder engine with B-metric deflation and passing focused engine/type regressions

title: geneig: geometry constructor for operator pair (A, B) + metric

Add geometry('geneig', A, B, metric) or equivalent, validated at construction time: A symmetric, B symmetric positive definite, same dimensions. This is the type-system entry point for the engine — nothing else can dispatch on a geneig shape until this exists.
acceptance_criteria: 1. R/geometry.R grows a geneig constructor that validates dimensions and symmetry of A and positive-definiteness of B. 2. Errors are specific: 'B must be symmetric positive definite; smallest eigenvalue = ...'. 3. typed_shape() composes geneig + generalized_eigen + a design kind without error. 4. infer_recipe() refuses geneig paired with any relation other than generalized_eigen. 5. test-geometry.R and test-typed-shape.R extend to cover the new constructor.
notes: Implemented geneig geometry constructor in R/geometry.R with optional validated (A, B, metric) payload, specific SPD failure message reporting smallest eigenvalue, and compile-time infer_recipe gate forcing geneig -> generalized_eigen. Extended geometry/typed-shape/infer-recipe tests and regenerated man/geometry.Rd. Verified with devtools::test(filter='geometry'), devtools::test(filter='typed-shape'), and devtools::test(filter='infer-recipe').

title: geneig spec: notes/engine_geneig_spec.md
description: Design note for the generalized-eigen engine using the six-part admissibility template. Must make the B-metric deflation explicit: the whitened operator is C = B^{-1/2} A B^{-1/2} but deflation happens in the B-metric, not Euclidean. The note decides which null families are valid for which sub-families (LDA uses label permutation; contrastive PCA uses sign flips; metric-weighted PCA is trickier and may stay planned-only in v1).
acceptance_criteria: 1. notes/engine_geneig_spec.md exists with the six-part template. 2. Explicitly specifies (a) ordered estimand as generalized eigenvalues; (b) deflation as B-orthogonal removal of the first k directions; (c) null families by sub-family (LDA: label permutation; contrastive PCA: sign flip on the negative term); (d) rung statistic as generalized-root tail ratio; (e) adapter-contract requirements (operator pair (A, B), metric-aware deflation hook); (f) fast-path plan deferred to Sprint 5 or later. 3. Declares LDA as the v1 flagship and contrastive PCA / metric-weighted PCA as future. 4. Cross-references architecture_rules.md Rule 1 (geneig as direct-latent-root engine, not oneblock branch).
close_reason: notes/engine_geneig_spec.md committed — covers all six admissibility sections. B-metric deflation is the hard rule (§2); LDA declared v1 flagship; null delegated to adapter per sub-family; B=I regression + Euclidean-vs-B-metric regression tests specified. Cites architecture_rules.md Rules 1 and 6.

title: PLSR: run_predictive_ladder() engine
description: New R/engine_predictive.R with run_predictive_ladder() implementing the same outer shell as run_oneblock_ladder and run_cross_ladder (ladder_driver + stop-at-first-non-rejection + form_units) but with a held-out / cross-fitted rung statistic and predictive deflation. Inputs: compiled recipe with (cross, predictive), X and Y matrices, fold vector or default K-fold generator. Null action is Y-row permutation; deflation removes the first k fitted latent predictors from the predictor construction, not from X^T Y.

Test-engine-predictive.R: planted rank-k supervised signal in X -> Y recovers k significant components with correct null calibration. 5. Negative test: the same data with Y shuffled returns p-values uniform under the null (FPR near alpha). 6. Refuses to run when the compiled recipe's relation is not 'predictive' (mirrors run_cross_ladder's relation check).

close_reason: Implemented run_predictive_ladder() as a shared-scaffold predictive engine using ladder_driver() and form_units(), with held-out fold-based incremental gain statistics, predictive relation guards, and predictive-engine tests covering planted supervised rank recovery and null calibration under shuffled Y. This is the reviewer's hard admissibility rule turned into registration-time enforcement.

R/adapter.R capability gate fails at infer_adapter() time when (cross, predictive, component_significance) is claimed without predict_response AND a split-aware component_stat. Another test case: a mock adapter with cross-fitted stat + predict_response is ACCEPTED. 5. Contract documentation vignette (writing-adapters.Rmd) gains a predictive-relation section.

No engine exists yet — registration of a predictive adapter without the Sprint 3 engine should still fail cleanly at recipe compile time.

title: PLSR spec: notes/engine_predictive_spec.md
description: Before any code lands, write the design note for the predictive engine using the six-part admissibility template (estimand / deflation / null / rung / contract / fast-path). title: Normalize cross covariance fit rank to active numerical tail
description: The cross core-space bootstrap study shows that the core-space and raw-refit bootstrap paths agree to machine precision on the leading latent units and on the reconstructed cross operator, but the raw refit path can retain a long numerical tail in wide low-n settings while the core path naturally stops at the active latent rank. This representation mismatch makes artifact dimensions and downstream singleton summaries disagree for trailing near-zero roots even though the substantive operator is the same. Follow-up: decide whether adapter_cross_svd refit/roots should trim numerically zero singular values, or whether unit formation/bootstrap comparison should normalize to an active-rank convention for cross covariance fits.

title: Bug: .restricted_row_permutation warns 'number of items to replace' on singleton groups
description: test-validity-checks.R:319 (permissive-mode grouped-design validity failures test) surfaces a pre-existing warning chain from .restricted_row_permutation when the groups factor has singleton levels. Reproduction: infer_cca(..., design = blocked_rows(rep(1, n))) or similar degenerate group fixture in permissive mode. The test still passes (warnings only, no error) but the unsplit(lapply(split(idx, groups), sample), groups) call emits 'number of items to replace is not a multiple of replacement length'. Likely fix: check for all-singleton groups in .restricted_row_permutation and short-circuit to the identity permutation with a single documented warning, or refuse earlier via checked_assumptions (cross_grouped_design_consistency already refuses singleton-only groups in strict mode, so the permissive path is the leak).
acceptance_criteria: 1. Reproduce the warning with a minimal fixture. 2. Fix either in .restricted_row_permutation (short-circuit) or in the permissive-mode path (skip null draws when exchangeability-group is degenerate). 3. test-validity-checks.R runs clean with zero warnings. 4. New test pinning the warning-free behaviour on the minimal fixture.
notes: Codex taking this now: reproduce the singleton-group warning in the grouped CCA permutation path, patch .restricted_row_permutation or the permissive null path, and add a warning-free regression test.
close_reason: Fixed singleton-group warnings in .restricted_row_permutation() by sampling group labels explicitly rather than calling sample() on length-1 numeric groups, and added a permissive infer_cca() regression test asserting warning-free behavior on a singleton-group blocked design. Verified with devtools::test(filter='validity-checks') and a direct warning-capture reproduction.
